title: Public hearing decision in the case of CDE
body: "A judicial Parole Board member, on behalf of the Board Chair, has not granted the application for CDE’s parole hearing to be made public.  Given that there is currently a court order in place preventing us from disclosing this prisoner’s identity and the identity of others, we have chosen to identify him using the initials ‘CDE’. Please note this order is binding on you too and you must therefore also refer to him as ‘CDE’.  More information about the reasons for this decision are attached.  For more detail on applying for a parole hearing to be made public, please read more here: Applying for a Parole review to be public - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) "
